title: Automatic Storage, Recursion, VLAs, and Escaping Addresses · Native Transfer Build
summary: An independent maintainer ports the bounded stack-use redesign to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded stack-use redesign with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep pointers to automatic objects within the execution and subobject lifetimes that make them valid.
Bound recursion by adversarial input depth, frame cost, and target stack limits or replace it with explicit storage.
Use variable-length arrays only under explicit positive bounds and stack budgets, with portable alternatives.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:

At Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Escape Analysis Review, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A lab native-systems team handles memory case 2243. Produce bounded C evidence to replace unbounded recursive work with bounded explicit storage; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Escape Analysis Review, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: Only return statements can cause a local address to escape.

stimulus.title: Escape Analysis Review failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Only return statements can cause a local address to escape.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.

options.text: Keep the plausible Escape Analysis Review output and hide the violated bounded stack-use redesign assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Escape Analysis Review.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Escape Analysis Review case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe. This does not satisfy Escape Analysis Review for bounded stack-use redesign.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Escape Analysis Review condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Escape Analysis Review
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Escape Analysis Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 9624. Produce bounded C evidence to replace unbounded recursive work with bounded explicit storage; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Escape Analysis Review.
steps.why: Retrieving Escape Analysis Review through the concrete bounded stack-use redesign case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Escape Analysis Review, keep Escape Analysis Review connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Trace every stored or returned pointer back to its referent lifetime, including compound literals and callback contexts.
